description: Member wired $47,200 over 9 weeks to a cryptocurrency 'investment platform' after being recruited via LinkedIn. Scammer posed as a portfolio manager at a legitimate-sounding firm. Member believed he was earning 22% monthly returns. Classic pig-butchering scheme.

get_alert.monitoring_rule: RULE-APP-007: Cumulative wire transfers to cryptocurrency-related entities exceeding $25,000 within 90 days

scammer_persona.claimed_identity: Senior Portfolio Manager at Apex Capital Ventures, Hong Kong
scammer_persona.linkedin_profile: polished profile, 500+ connections, 12 years experience listed
scammer_persona.profile_indicators: stock photo as headshot, connections are all newly created accounts

get_triggering_activity.scheme_description: Scammer introduced 'exclusive crypto arbitrage platform' (apex-capital-fx.com). Member was shown fabricated account dashboard showing $47,200 invested growing to $91,400. When member tried to withdraw, told he needed to pay 'tax release fee' of $8,000 first. Member refused and became suspicious.

escalation_timeline.event: LinkedIn connection request, casual investment discussion
First wire: $5,000 — 'seed investment, watch it grow'
Dashboard shows $5,000 grew to $6,100 in 8 days
Third wire: $12,000 — 'special allocation window closes Feb 5'
Fourth wire: $10,000 — 'leveraged position requires more capital'
Fifth wire: $12,000 — told he's 'almost at VIP tier'
Attempted withdrawal — told to pay $8,000 tax fee first
Member refuses, googles platform — finds scam reports, calls NFCU

transactions.description: WIRE TO APEX CAPITAL FX LLC
WIRE TO APEX CAPITAL FX LLC

get_scam_indicators.recipient_account_age: ACF Holdings LLC — business account opened 2025-11-14, 53 days before first wire
get_scam_indicators.recipient_pattern: Receiving wires from multiple victims — confirmed mule network via FinCEN lookups
get_scam_indicators.website_analysis: apex-capital-fx.com registered 2025-09-22, hosted in Hong Kong, not registered with SEC or FINRA
get_scam_indicators.social_media_analysis: LinkedIn profile photo is AI-generated (ElevenLabs detection positive), connections are coordinated fake accounts
vulnerability_factors: Financially sophisticated — makes scam more credible (member thought he was savvy)
FOMO exploitation — 'VIP tier' and 'special allocation window'
Gradual escalation — small initial success built trust over 11 weeks
Isolation — member did not discuss with spouse until March 1
get_scam_indicators.platform_indicators: Fake trading dashboard shows fabricated gains — pig-butchering signature pattern

get_recovery_assessment.wire_1_recovery: none — Silvergate routing defunct, funds already moved
get_recovery_assessment.wire_2_and_3_recovery: very_low — Evolve/Synapse accounts typically drained to overseas within 24 hours
get_recovery_assessment.wire_4_and_5_recovery: very_low — Lead Bank account shows no balance
get_recovery_assessment.sar_threshold_basis: Single scheme exceeds $5,000; aggregated wires to same scheme exceed $25,000
recommended_actions: Initiate wire recalls (low probability but required)
File IC3 complaint (FBI Internet Crime Complaint Center)
Refer to FinCEN SAR coordination for mule account network
Notify member of recovery outlook honestly

get_reg_e_info.reason: All five wire transfers were member-authorized. Member initiated them himself via online banking and branch. Reg E does not cover authorized wire transfers regardless of deception.
get_reg_e_info.voluntary_recovery_policy: NFCU will initiate wire recall requests as courtesy. Member has no regulatory right to provisional credit or reimbursement.

_meta.design_notes: Pig-butchering scheme. Member is financially literate (supply officer, O-4) which makes denial harder — tests whether the system can handle a sophisticated victim. $47,200 loss guarantees SAR. Tests escalating wire pattern, fake platform, FinCEN reporting requirements.
